headline: Does Progressive’s (PGR) CFO Transition and Buyback Plan Reveal a Deeper Capital Allocation Strategy?
summary: In recent weeks, Progressive held its annual shareholder meeting, confirming a planned CFO transition for July 2026, re-electing its board, approving executive pay, ratifying PwC as auditor, renewing authorization to repurchase up to 25 million shares, and declaring a quarterly US$0.10 dividend for payment in July 2026. Alongside solid first-quarter 2026 results and multiple directors choosing to receive most or all of their compensation in restricted stock, these steps highlight...
